---
title: "Geospatial / GIS & Built Environment"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} · **Methods & Study Design** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Methods & Study Design track](methods-study-design.qmd){.track-back}

*AI support for the spatial methods behind built-environment and environmental-health research.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Use AI to scaffold mapping and spatial-join code
- Work with street-level and satellite imagery responsibly
- Validate spatial results and watch for ecological fallacy

## Overview

AI can scaffold geospatial code (mapping, buffers, spatial joins) and help interpret street-level or satellite imagery — directly useful for built-environment and environmental-health work. As with all coding, you read and validate what it produces.

Spatial work has its own traps: projection errors, edge effects, and the ecological fallacy. AI won't catch these for you; methodological judgment does.

## Practice activities

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Activity 1 · Advanced — Scaffold a spatial join

1. Describe two **public** spatial datasets and the join you want.
2. Ask AI for the code (e.g., R sf or Python geopandas) and **explain each step**.
3. Run it; validate against a known case.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Activity 2 · Advanced — Interpret imagery carefully

1. Use AI to describe features in a **public** street/satellite image.
2. Note where it guessed, and what a human rater must confirm.
:::

## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "AI-written geospatial code should be…", "options": ["Read and validated against a known case", "Trusted if it runs", "Published immediately", "Ignored"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Validation catches projection and join errors."}, {"q": "The ecological fallacy is…", "options": ["Inferring individual traits from area-level data", "A mapping bug", "A file format", "A model type"], "answer": 0, "explain": "A classic spatial-analysis trap AI won't catch for you."}, {"q": "Imagery interpretation by AI needs…", "options": ["Human confirmation of what it guessed", "No review", "A bigger model only", "Nothing"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Vision models guess; you verify."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **New Earth Observation Tech Is Boosting Climate Intelligence (WEF)** — satellite data for environmental health.
- **Charting the Future of Earth Observation (WEF, 2024)** — where geospatial AI is heading.

## Useful resources

- [QGIS](https://qgis.org) — free, open-source GIS.
- [geopandas](https://geopandas.org) — spatial analysis in Python.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Use **public** spatial data and imagery; avoid uploading images with identifiable people to unapproved tools. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
